title: GREEN: implement /ruta-help mode-aware default and unknown-topic fallback

title: GREEN: implement shared disclosure model and /ruta-status output

acceptance_criteria: Implement shared disclosure helper getModeCommandDisclosure(state) with groups always/bootstrap/mode-specific/transition; /ruta-status scratch output includes 'Available now' and 'Next unlock'; degraded-state path shows recovery hint; previously failing ruta-status and shared-model tests pass.

title: RED: add failing tests for /ruta-status command disclosure

acceptance_criteria: Failing tests added for /ruta-status disclosure: (1) per-mode 'Available now' section, (2) 'Next unlock' includes unsatisfied gate + transition command; tests fail before implementation and run via npm test.

title: RED: add failing tests for /ruta-help disclosure and invariants

acceptance_criteria: Failing tests added for /ruta-help: (1) no-topic mode-aware map, (2) /ruta-help <topic> unchanged topic behavior, (3) unknown-topic fallback suggests valid topics and returns mode-aware map, (4) disclosure does not alter command authorization behavior; tests fail before implementation and run via npm test.
notes: Expected touchpoints: help topic resolution + fallback behavior.

title: Rewrite /ruta-init for active session handling
description:
**Context:** Rewrite `/ruta-init` and existing handlers to use the active-session model.
Ref: `openspec/changes/add-session-per-spec-layout/tasks.md` §2.2.1-§2.2.4

**Files:**
- `extensions/ruta.ts`
- `extensions/state.ts`
- `test/**`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `/ruta-init` computes UUID, discovers existing sessions, and prompts for resume vs fresh when appropriate
- [ ] `/ruta-init` scaffolds or resumes the correct session directory
- [ ] The active PID entry is written to `.ruta/active.json` on activation
- [ ] Parallel access to the same session emits a warning without blocking
- [ ] All handlers use the active-session loader and `sessionDir`-based save/load helpers

---
**CRITICAL: Follow Test Driven Development and Tidy First workflows.**
- Write tests *before* writing implementation code.
- Clean up related code *before* adding new functionality.
notes:
OpenSpec refs: add-session-per-spec-layout:2.2.1,2.2.2,2.2.3,2.2.4
Verification: run command-flow tests covering /ruta-init, then npm test.

**Files:**
- `extensions/ruta.ts`

- `test/**`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Register `/ruta-resume` with argument completion for spec paths
- [ ] `/ruta-resume <path>` resumes the latest session without prompting for fresh vs resume
- [ ] `/ruta-resume` with no args lists all existing sessions and activates the selected session
- [ ] Register `/ruta-switch` and implement session picker behavior
- [ ] `/ruta-switch` shows the required no-session guidance when no sessions exist

---
**CRITICAL: Follow Test Driven Development and Tidy First workflows.**
- Write tests *before* writing implementation code.
- Clean up related code *before* adding new functionality.
notes:
OpenSpec refs: add-session-per-spec-layout:2.2.5,2.2.6
Verification: run command-flow tests covering /ruta-resume and /ruta-switch, then npm test.

title: Implement state layer for session-per-spec layout
description:
**Context:** Implement the state-layer primitives required by the session-per-spec workspace layout.
Ref: `openspec/changes/add-session-per-spec-layout/tasks.md` §2.1.1-§2.1.9

**Files:**
- `extensions/state.ts`
- `test/**`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Implement `computeSpecUUID(cwd, specPath)`
- [ ] Implement `readActiveJson(cwd)` / `writeActiveJson(cwd, map)` with dead-PID pruning
- [ ] Implement `writeActiveEntry(cwd, uuid, sessionId, sourcePath)`
- [ ] Implement `loadActiveSession(cwd)` returning `{ state, sessionDir, active } | null`
- [ ] Implement `listAllSessions(cwd)` for all known sessions
- [ ] Refactor `artifactPaths(cwd)` to `artifactPaths(sessionDir)`
- [ ] Refactor `saveProjectState(cwd, state)` to `saveProjectState(sessionDir, state)`
- [ ] Implement `scaffoldSession(cwd, sourcePath, sessionDir)`
- [ ] Preserve `source_spec_path` persistence in state

---
**CRITICAL: Follow Test Driven Development and Tidy First workflows.**
- Write tests *before* writing implementation code.
- Clean up related code *before* adding new functionality.

OpenSpec refs: add-session-per-spec-layout:2.1.1,2.1.2,2.1.3,2.1.4,2.1.5,2.1.6,2.1.7,2.1.8,2.1.9

title: Enforce triage token lifecycle for ruta_add_gap
description:
Review findings CORR-003 and issue-review SCOPE-001/EXEC-001.

Extract the authorization and lifecycle rules for triage tokens into a focused ticket so the behavioral contract is correct before or alongside any richer triage UI work.

Spec refs: R-6.5.1, R-6.5.2
Files: extensions/ruta.ts, extensions/state.ts, test/*.ts, README.md
Source: 2026-04-21 Rule-of-5 implementation review + 2026-04-21 issue-review follow-up.
acceptance_criteria:
- add failing tests for triage token creation, validation, and invalidation
- ruta_add_gap succeeds only with a live token from the active triage flow
- closing or dismissing triage invalidates the token
- manual /ruta-add-gap remains a separate path and is documented as such
- manual verification: run /ruta-probe <section>, confirm accepted triage can append a gap, close triage, then confirm the same token no longer works
- npm test passes

title: Implement real gap triage UI for ruta-probe and ruta_add_gap
description:
Review findings DRAFT-001 plus issue-review SCOPE-001.

Build the dedicated triage UI for /ruta-probe after the underlying authorization semantics are in place. Focus this ticket on user-visible triage affordances rather than token lifecycle internals.

Spec refs: R-5.4.2, R-6.5.1
Files: extensions/ruta.ts, extensions/spec-viewer.ts or new triage UI module, test/*.ts, README.md
Source: 2026-04-21 Rule-of-5 implementation review + 2026-04-21 issue-review follow-up.
acceptance_criteria:
- add failing tests for triage UI rendering and explicit accept/discard affordances where testable
- /ruta-probe opens a dedicated triage UI instead of a generic scratch editor
- the triage UI exposes explicit accept/discard actions for proposed gap lines
- accepted findings use the authorized gap-append path from the active triage flow
- manual /ruta-add-gap remains clearly separate from triage UI actions
- manual verification: run /ruta-probe <section>, inspect accept/discard flow, append an accepted gap, and confirm the result lands in gaps.md
- npm test passes

title: Fix read gate false positive from scaffold notebook content
description:
Review finding CORR-001.

The seeded notebook template currently satisfies read gate checks without a real user-authored note. Update the read gate so scaffold text does not count as progress.

Files: extensions/state.ts, test/state.test.ts, README.md
Source: 2026-04-21 Rule-of-5 implementation review + 2026-04-21 issue-review follow-up.
acceptance_criteria:
- add a failing test showing the scaffolded notebook plus unity sentence does not satisfy the read gate
- readGateSatisfied only passes when there is real user-authored notebook content
- ruta-init scaffold behavior and docs stay coherent with the new rule
- manual verification: /ruta-init ruta-spec-v0.2.md, set a unity sentence, then confirm /ruta-done-reading still refuses until a real note is added
- npm test passes

title: Make reimplement gate honor declared scope
description:
Review finding CORR-002.

/ruta-scope persists state.scope, but the reimplement gate ignores it and counts all major headings in the spec. Make the gate evaluate only headings within the declared scope.

Spec refs: §5.4 scope behavior and gate counting within declared scope
Files: extensions/ruta.ts, extensions/state.ts, test/state.test.ts, README.md
Source: 2026-04-21 Rule-of-5 implementation review + 2026-04-21 issue-review follow-up.
acceptance_criteria:
- add failing tests for scoped and unscoped reimplement gate evaluation
- define and test supported scope syntax/validation for the declared scope used by the gate
- /ruta-done-reimplement uses the declared scope from .ruta/ruta.json
- heading counting within scope matches documented behavior
- manual verification: set a narrow scope with /ruta-scope, add gaps only for in-scope sections, then confirm /ruta-done-reimplement succeeds without covering out-of-scope sections
- npm test passes

title: Add /ruta-tutorial and tighten mode skill scoping
description: Implement approved onboarding command and tighten mode-specific skill guidance so current-step discovery is clearer.

title: Add prompt-override detection and startup warning
description:
Review finding CORR-005.

ruta warns on prompt bundle hash drift, but does not detect known external prompt-override patterns during session start as required by the spec. Add the detection-and-warn layer and cover it with tests.

Spec ref: R-7.1.3
Files: extensions/ruta.ts, prompts-version.txt, package.json or new prompt-integrity asset file, test/*.ts, README.md
Source: 2026-04-21 Rule-of-5 implementation review + 2026-04-21 issue-review follow-up.
acceptance_criteria:
- add failing tests for detection of known-dangerous override patterns
- session start warns when a known external prompt override pattern is detected
- the known-pattern list ships with the package and is versioned with prompt-related assets
- existing prompt hash drift warning continues to work
- manual verification: start a session with a fixture/mock representing an override pattern and confirm the warning is shown
- npm test passes

title: Bring ruta-disagree into spec-compliant disagreement workflow
description:
Review findings DRAFT-002 and CORR-004.

/ruta-disagree currently replays the last prompt to the first different-provider model and dumps both responses sequentially. Implement the documented workflow: configurable secondary model selection, disagreement-aware presentation, and route-to-gap-probe guidance when claims diverge.

Spec refs: R-8.1.1, R-8.1.2, R-8.1.3
Files: extensions/ruta.ts, extensions/prompts.ts, test/*.ts, README.md
Source: 2026-04-21 Rule-of-5 implementation review + 2026-04-21 issue-review follow-up.
acceptance_criteria:
- add failing tests for secondary-model selection and disagreement handling behavior
- /ruta-disagree honors configured secondary model selection when available
- disagreements produce a structured comparison and suggest ruta_gap_probe on the relevant section
- agreements still show the reminder that agreement is not evidence of spec clarity
- single-provider behavior remains informative and non-destructive
- manual verification: test /ruta-disagree with two configured providers and again with only one provider available
- npm test passes

title: Harden section resolution for viewer and gap probing
description:
Review findings EDGE-001 and EDGE-002.

Section lookup currently falls back to the entire spec on misses and uses substring matching that can silently land on the wrong heading. Make section targeting exact-or-unambiguous and fail fast on missing/ambiguous refs.

Files: extensions/spec-viewer.ts, extensions/state.ts, test/spec-viewer.test.ts, test/state.test.ts, README.md
Source: 2026-04-21 Rule-of-5 implementation review + 2026-04-21 issue-review follow-up.
acceptance_criteria:
- add failing tests for missing and ambiguous section references
- viewer jumps prefer exact normalized heading matches
- probing a missing or ambiguous section returns a user-facing error instead of the whole spec
- fuzzy matching is allowed only when unique and documented
- manual verification: /ruta-open-spec Goals, /ruta-open-spec with an ambiguous query, and /ruta-probe with a missing section ref
- npm test passes

title: Address Rule-of-5 review findings
description:
Address the implementation gaps found in the 2026-04-21 Rule-of-5-universal review.

Context:
- Review covered extensions/ruta.ts, extensions/state.ts, extensions/spec-viewer.ts, test/*.ts, README.md
- Goal is spec compliance and correctness before further feature expansion
- Follow TDD and Tidy First: write the failing test first, make it pass, then do cleanup in a separate step/commit
- Source: 2026-04-21 Rule-of-5 implementation review + 2026-04-21 issue-review follow-up.

Epic done when:
- all remediation child tickets are closed or explicitly deferred with rationale
- automated tests pass
- spec-required behavior covered by these findings is implemented or explicitly documented as deferred
- follow-up review no longer reports these gaps as unresolved
acceptance_criteria:
- all child tickets are closed or explicitly deferred with rationale
- npm test passes on the final remediation branch
- remediation covers read gate correctness, scope-aware reimplement gating, triage correctness, disagree workflow, section targeting, and prompt-override detection
- any intentionally deferred behavior is documented in README/spec notes

title: Add cursor-anchored comments in the spec viewer
description: Use the sidecar comment model from Phase 1 to create and list comments from the viewer.
acceptance_criteria:
- alt+c adds a comment at the current line
- comment persists to .ruta/comments.json with anchor metadata
- /ruta-comments lists stored comments
- comments survive restart and spec remains unchanged

title: Add chorded shortcuts and comment navigation polish
description: Polish the viewer with chord support and comment navigation.
acceptance_criteria:
- ctrl+k ctrl+c works inside the custom viewer
- next/previous comment navigation exists
- edit/delete flows are available or explicitly deferred
- status text documents available keybindings

title: Add sidecar model and persistence for ruta spec comments
description: Implement the Phase 1 data layer in extensions/state.ts and test/state.test.ts.
acceptance_criteria:
- define .ruta/comments.json format in code
- round-trip comment data via state helpers
- append preserves existing comments
- anchor helper returns best-effort section metadata and excerpt
- automated tests cover persistence and anchor derivation
notes: Started implementation. Added .ruta/comments.json helpers, comment anchor derivation, and tests for round-trip, append, and anchor behavior. npm test passes.
close_reason: Completed and committed Phase 1 comment sidecar data layer in 787e32f with tests.

title: Add read-only ruta spec viewer command
description: Implement /ruta-open-spec and a read-only in-pi spec viewer.
acceptance_criteria:
- /ruta-open-spec opens the current spec inside pi
- viewer supports cursor movement and line numbers
- no writes occur to spec/
- helper logic is covered by tests or documented smoke tests
notes:
Beginning Phase 2. Will add pure viewer helpers with tests first, then wire /ruta-open-spec to a local read-only custom component.
Implemented a read-only /ruta-open-spec viewer. Added pure helpers for section lookup and framed line rendering with tests, wired a local custom TUI component with cursor navigation and line numbers, and documented manual smoke-test steps in README.md. npm run check passes.
close_reason: Completed and committed read-only spec viewer in 7cbed31 with tests and README smoke steps.

title: Spec viewer and cursor-anchored comments
